description_long: This object is a carved stone guard stone, featuring a stylized humanoid figure with exaggerated facial features and a prominent headpiece. The figure stands upright with its arms bent and hands resting on its thighs, and its legs are slightly apart, forming a narrow opening at the base. The surface is rough and weathered, with visible chipping and erosion, especially on the head and extremities. The stone is a light tan to brown color, with some darker discoloration in recessed areas, likely due to age and exposure. The overall form is blocky and robust, suggesting it was designed to withstand impact or serve as a protective barrier. There are no visible inscriptions or detailed engravings, but the sculptural style hints at historical or cultural significance.
description: A weathered, carved stone guard stone depicting a stylized humanoid figure with rough, tan surfaces and visible chipping. The figure stands upright, arms bent, and legs apart, exuding a robust and protective presence.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full humanoid figure, with facial features, headpiece, and both arms and legs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the profile of the head and body, showing the depth of the carving and the protruding facial features.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the figure, with less detail and visible weathering along the spine and legs.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, emphasizing the blocky form and the angularity of the head and limbs.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to driveways, alleyways, or historical sites, often flanking gateways or marking the corners of urban streets to protect structures from vehicle impact.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed upright, partially embedded in the ground for stability, with the carved figure facing outward toward approaching traffic or pedestrians.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and erosion on the head and extremities, with surface smoothing on protruding areas due to repeated contact and weathering. Discoloration and darkening in recessed areas likely caused by accumulated dirt and moisture.
